1200000157.english: So, the last person who came to record the voice data... was me?
1200000157.korean: 그래서, 음성 데이터를 녹음하러 마지막으로 찾아온 게… 나라고?

1200000159.english: You said it was animal sounds.

1200000161.english: ...But why are you coming to find me?

1200000163.english: JangHwa, is that what you were curious about?

1200000164.english: If you think about it carefully, maybe you can figure out the reason, meow~?
1200000164.korean: 자알 생각해보면 이유를 알 수 있지 않을까냥~?

1200000165.english: Get out before I kill you.

1200000166.english: No, but... I need to fill 20 voice samples, and I don't have any more animals to record!
1200000166.korean: 아니, 그치마안~ 보이스 샘플을 20개는 채워야 하는데 이젠 더 녹음할 동물이 없잖아!

1200000167.english: Why don't you try being cute and saying "nyan-nyan" instead?
1200000167.korean: 네가 대신 냥냥 하면서 애교라도 좀 부려 봐!

1200000169.english: If that's the case, why don't you at least search the Ark database! There must be a lot of animal voice data there!
1200000169.korean: 그런 거면 방주 데이터베이스라도 뒤져보던가! 거기에는 동물 음성 데이터 많을 거 아냐!

1200000171.english: I was just a little shocked.

1200000175.english: ...I've never been so humiliated. I feel like I want to crawl into a hole and hide.
1200000175.korean: …이렇게 수치스러울 데가. 쥐구멍이 있으면 들어가고 싶을 정도다.

1200000176.english: I really want to shove a bomb down both of their throats.
1200000176.korean: 진짜 둘 다 주둥이에 폭탄 하나씩 쑤셔 박고 싶네.

1200000177.english: If you don't want your snack money confiscated, watch your mouth, JangHwa. Don't you know it's the week for using kind and beautiful language? The use of profanity is prohibited.
1200000177.korean: 간식비를 몰수당하고 싶지 않다면 말 조심해라, 장화. 지금은 바르고 고운 말을 쓰는 주간인 걸 모르고 있는 거냐? 욕설은 사용 금지다.

1200000178.english: The mouth isn't for cursing, is it?

1200000180.english: To be criticized by JangHwa twice, such humiliation...
1200000180.korean: 장화에게 두 번이나 지적을 당하다니, 이런 치욕을...

1200000181.english: Are you thinking about being cloned? The way you are now, it might be better to just blow you up and start over.
1200000181.korean: 너 혹시 복제 될 생각은 없어? 지금의 너는 그냥 터뜨려 죽여버리고 새로 만드는 편이 나을 것 같은데.

1200000182.english: I won't refuse, as it would make my master sad.

1200000183.english: Anyway, the way you wriggle out of things...

1200000184.english: Anyway... as JangHwa said, I can just download the voice data from the Ark database and take it with me.
1200000184.korean: 어쨌거나… 장화 말대로 방주 데이터베이스에서 음성 데이터 다운받아서 가져가면 되겠다.

1200000185.english: Then this solves the problem! Thanks, both of you! I'll buy you something delicious later!
1200000185.korean: 그럼 이걸로 문제 해결이네! 고마워, 둘 다! 나중에 내가 맛있는 거 사 줄게!

1200000188.english: Why are you making that serious face again?

1200000189.english: No... now that I think about it, that animal language translator... doesn't it have some problems?
1200000189.korean: 아니… 이제 와서 든 생각인데, 그 동물 언어 번역기라는 거… 문제가 좀 있지 않나?

1200000191.english: ...A language translator, after all, needs to be able to make sounds with its mouth, doesn't it?
1200000191.korean: …언어 번역기라는 건, 결국 입으로 소리를 낼 수 있어야 하는 것 아닌가.

1200000192.english: Come to think of it, you're right.

1200000193.english: Can snakes make sounds with their mouths...?
1200000193.korean: 뱀이 입으로 소리를 낼 수 있던가…?

1200000195.english: ...I can't help it. Because I don't have vocal cords...

1200000197.english: ...Yes, I realized I was having the same problem, so I was looking for Mr. CheonA to tell her that we don't need to collect the data.
1200000197.korean: …네, 저도 그런 문제가 있는 걸 깨달아서, 데이터를 수집하지 않아도 된다고 말씀드리려고 천아 씨를 찾아다니고 있었어요.

1200000198.english: I'm sorry for causing you unnecessary trouble, even tho it wasn't intentional.
1200000198.korean: 본의 아니게 하지 않아도 되는 고생을 시켜드린 것 같아 죄송스럽네요.

1200000200.english: Well, since it's come to this, there's nothing we can do. Okay. These are the data I've collected so far... and the materials I downloaded from the Ark.
1200000200.korean: …뭐, 이렇게 된 이상 어쩔 수 없지. 자. 그 동안 모은 데이터랑… 방주에서 다운로드 받은 자료들이야.

1200000201.english: Please make a good translator. Even if I can't use it, others might be able to use it well.
1200000201.korean: 번역기 잘 만들어 줘. 나는 못 쓰더라도 다른 사람들은 잘 쓸 수 있겠지.

1200000202.english: No, this time it was my mistake, so... I'll definitely create a translator that can interpret the language of snakes.
1200000202.korean: 아뇨, 이번 일은 제 실수였으니까… 제가 꼭 뱀의 언어도 해석할 수 있는 번역기를 만들어 볼게요.

1200000203.english: The mistake was collecting only audio data when we were supposed to be translating animal languages in the first place. When communicating with animals, behavioral language is just as important a factor to consider as spoken language.
1200000203.korean: 애초에 동물의 언어를 번역한다면서 음성 데이터만 수집했던 게 잘못이었어요. 동물과 소통을 하기 위해서는 음성 언어만큼이나 행동 언어도 중요하게 고려해야 할 요소인데 말이죠.

1200000204.english: Then, if we put the data for body language into the translator as well...
1200000204.korean: 그럼, 행동 언어의 데이터까지 번역기에 집어넣으면…

1200000205.english: Error! Error! There's too much data!
1200000205.korean: 에러! 에러! 데이터 양이 너무 많습니다!

1200000206.english: Detecting more data than the storage device's capacity. Activating self-destruct sequence!
1200000206.korean: 저장 장치의 용량보다 더 많은 데이터 감지. 자폭 시퀀스를 가동합니다!

1200000208.english: Oh, since the device is small, I was worried if the self-destruct mechanism would work properly, but thankfully, the self-destruct sequence is activating without any issues.
1200000208.korean: 아, 장치가 소형이라 자폭 장치가 잘 작동할지 걱정했는데, 다행히 문제 없이 자폭 시퀀스가 발동하네요.

1200000209.english: Why is there self-destruct in the translator?

1200000210.english: They say the desire to destroy can lead to the desire to create, right? It has a self-destruct function to maximize your creative drive.
1200000210.korean: 파괴하려는 욕망은 창조욕으로 이어진다는 말도 있잖아요? 창작 욕구를 극대화하기 위해서 자폭 기능을 넣어둔 거랍니다.

1200000211.english: It will explode in 60 seconds. Please evacuate if you are nearby. 60, 59, 58...
1200000211.korean: 60초 후에 폭발합니다. 근처에 계신 분들은 대피해 주십시오. 60, 59, 58…

1200000213.english: I think I'll have to give up on the translator.

1200000214.english: Huh? You don't need to do that, CheonA. If we just increase the capacity of the data storage device, we'll definitely be able to create an excellent translator.
1200000214.korean: 네? 그러실 필요 없어요, 천아 씨. 데이터 저장 장치의 용량만 늘리면 분명 훌륭한 번역기를 만들 수 있을 거예요.

1200000215.english: Are you going to remove the self-destruct device?

1200000216.english: Let me think about that for a moment...

1200000218.english: ...I guess it's better to quit after all.

1200000219.english: Mr. CheonA? Where are you going? Mr. CheonA?
1200000219.korean: 천아 씨? 어디 가세요? 천아 씨?

1200000220.english: Please wait a moment. I haven't finished thinking yet.
1200000220.korean: 잠시만 기다려 주세요. 아직 생각이 끝나지 않았어요.

1200000225.english: Why was there an explosion sound coming from my room...?

1200000228.english: What's the reason this time...? Haha...

1200000230.english: How many times is this now?! I won't let you get away with this! Azaz!! Come out right now!!!
1200000230.korean: 이게 대체 몇 번째야! 가만두지 않겠어! 아자즈!! 당장 나와!!!

1200000232.english: And so, the curtain fell on the .
1200000232.korean: 그렇게 <동물 언어 번역기 소동>은 막을 내렸습니다.

1200000233.english: ...Yeah, it was a truly ridiculous incident.

1200000234.english: In the end, I did manage to catch Azaz and make her repair the room, but... I couldn't sleep properly during that time.
1200000234.korean: 결국 아자즈를 잡아서 방을 수리하게 시키긴 했지만… 그 동안 난 잠도 제대로 못 잤다고.

1200000235.english: Still, now that I have a wall strong enough that it won't collapse even if a bomb goes off, I guess you could say it's a happy ending in its own way...
1200000235.korean: 그래도 이제는 폭탄이 터져도 무너지지 않을 정도로 튼튼한 벽이 생겼으니까, 뭐 나름대로 해피엔딩이라고 할 수 있지 않을까 싶네…

1200000236.english: Oh my, Dracula! You're really positive.

1200000237.english: ...because I've been so tormented by you guys, I've learned that if I don't think positively like this, I can't manage my stress.
1200000237.korean: …너희한테 하도 시달리다 보니까, 이렇게라도 긍정적으로 생각하지 않으면 스트레스 관리가 안 된다는 사실을 배웠거든.

1200000238.english: However, there was one thing Dracula knew that she didn't.
1200000238.korean: 하지만, 드라큐리나가 모르고 있는 사실이 하나 있었습니다.

1200000240.english: The "Animal Language Translator Incident" had come to an end, but what Draculina didn't know was that an even bigger commotion was waiting for her just around the corner...
1200000240.korean: <동물 언어 번역기 소동>은 막을 내렸지만, 그 뒤에 이어질 더 큰 소동이 드라큐리나를 기다리고 있었다는 것을요…

1200000241.english: Wait a minute, what's that? I've never heard that before. What did you guys do this time?
1200000241.korean: 잠깐만, 뭔데? 난 처음 듣는 얘긴데? 너희 또 무슨 짓 했어?

1200000242.english: Okay, let's continue that story next time.
1200000242.korean: 그럼, 그 이야기는 다음 시간에 계속 하도록 하겠습니다.

1200000243.english: You all need to stay alive until I tell you the next story.
1200000243.korean: 다음 이야기를 들려드릴 때까지, 죽지 않고 살아 계셔야 해요, 여러분.

1200000244.english: Just a moment! Hey! Wait! Finish explaining and then go!
1200000244.korean: 잠깐만! 야! 기다려! 마저 설명하고 가!

1200000245.english: What's the bigger commotion that's coming next? What is Azaz doing again?!
1200000245.korean: 다음에 이어질 더 큰 소동이 뭔데? 아자즈가 또 무슨 짓을 하는 건데?!

1200000248.english: Captain, Kali. You trust me, right?

1200000249.english: ...Is that the first thing you say after calling the captain, who was in the middle of enjoying her vacation, and saying you have a "huge plan"?
1200000249.korean: …그게 한창 휴가를 즐기던 대장을 ‘엄청난 계획이 있다’면서 불러내고서 꺼내는 첫 마디냐?

1200000250.english: You know that's a really scammy line, right? I was getting ready to go to the water park, but if you're trying to scam me, you're dead.
1200000250.korean: 그거 엄청 사기꾼 같은 멘트인거 알지? 워터파크 가려고 준비중이었는데, 너 나한테 사기 치는 거면 진짜 죽는다.

1200000251.english: Hey, I'm not a salamander, so why would I deceive my own comrades? She said she called because she had a great business idea and wanted to give me a heads-up.
1200000251.korean: 에헤이, 내가 뭐 샐러맨더도 아닌데 같은 부대원들에게 사기를 칠 리가 없잖아? 엄청난 사업 아이템이 있어서 슬쩍 알려주려고 부른 거라고.

1200000252.english: And you'll be in big trouble if you wear that to a water park.
1200000252.korean: 그리고 그거 입고 워터파크 가면 큰일 난다.

1200000253.english: It's starting to sound more and more like a scammer's line...
1200000253.korean: 점점 더 사기꾼 같은 멘트가 되어가는데…

1200000254.english: Wait, wait! Just listen and decide!

1200000255.english: What I'm trying to do is...

1200000256.english: That's right, I'll be broadcasting thru the newly revamped Orca Secret Network!
1200000256.korean: 바로 이번에 새롭게 개편되는 오르카 시크릿 네트워크를 통해 방송을 하는 거야!

1200000259.english: It's too normal a suggestion for you.

1200000260.english: That's really too much. Don't you trust your subordinates that much?
1200000260.korean: 너무하네 정말. 부하에 대한 신뢰가 그렇게 없어?

1200000261.english: The panther believes. I trust Io, and I trust Calista...
1200000261.korean: 팬서는 믿지. 이오도 믿고, 칼리스타도 믿는데…

1200000262.english: ...Honestly, I'm a little worried about you. Fake news? I don't remember raising you like that...
1200000262.korean: …솔직히 넌 좀 걱정된다. 가짜 뉴스라니, 내가 널 그렇게 키운 기억이 없는데…

1200000263.english: Fake news, you say! It's a news story based purely on facts!
1200000263.korean: 가짜 뉴스라니! 엄연히 팩트를 기반으로 한 뉴스야!

1200000264.korean: …단지 몇 가지 양념을 좀 쳤을 뿐이지.

1200000265.english: We call that fake news, Spriggan.
1200000265.korean: 우린 그걸 가짜 뉴스라고 한단다, 스프리건아.

1200000267.english: And honestly, if you're talking about the person who raised us, it would be the Panther's deputy commander, not the commander.
1200000267.korean: 그리고 솔직히 우릴 키운 사람이라고 하면 대장이 아니라 팬서 부대장이겠지.

1200000269.english: As expected, Panther is the leader material. So, why did you suddenly think of that broadcast?
1200000269.korean: 역시 팬서가 대장감이라니까. 그래서, 갑자기 그 방송 생각은 왜 하게 된 건데?

1200000270.english: Well, you see... while I was looking thru the Ark's records, I came across something that said in the world before the destruction, they broadcast over the internet and made a lot of money doing it!
1200000270.korean: 그건 말이지… 방주의 기록을 뒤지다가 보게 된 건데, 멸망 전의 세상에서는 인터넷을 통해 방송을 했고, 그걸로 엄청난 돈을 벌기도 했대!

1200000272.english: Now that we're connected to Caracas and have completely replaced the intranet with the internet, right? So, you broadcast the show worldwide thru that.
1200000272.korean: 이번에 카라카스랑 네트워크가 연결되면서 인트라넷을 인터넷으로 싹 교체했잖아? 그러니까, 그걸 통해서 방송을 전 세계로 송출하는 거지.

1200000274.english: That's a good idea. The potential of the internet broadcasting market is quite significant.
1200000274.korean: 괜찮은 아이디어네. 인터넷 방송 시장의 가능성은 꽤 크니까.

1200000275.english: Based on past examples, I think tuna cans could also be quite profitable... It's an interesting proposal, but...
1200000275.korean: 과거의 사례를 기반으로 생각했을 때, 참치캔도 꽤 벌 수 있을 것 같고… 흥미로운 제안이긴 한데…

1200000276.english: ...Why did you come looking for me? I don't really have anything to do with broadcasting.
1200000276.korean: …왜 날 찾아온 거냐? 난 방송 쪽이랑은 별로 연관이 없는데.

1200000277.english: Me too, you know. Wouldn't it be better to go find Talon Feather or orangeade?
1200000277.korean: 나도 그렇고 말이야. 탈론페더나 오렌지에이드를 찾아가는 편이 낫지 않겠어?

1200000278.english: Oh, I came to ask you two for an investment. The boss is too lazy to even use tuna cans, she just collects them all, right? Calista is also quite diligent about saving.
1200000278.korean: 아, 두 사람한테는 투자를 부탁하려고 찾아 온 거야. 대장은 참치캔 쓰기도 귀찮다고 싹 다 모으기만 하잖아? 칼리스타도 꽤 열심히 저축을 하는 편이고.

1200000279.english: Well, I have definitely collected quite a bit...
1200000279.korean: 뭐, 확실히 꽤 많이 모아놓긴 했지…

1200000280.english: Have you thot about what kind of content you'll do for your broadcast?
1200000280.korean: 방송 컨텐츠로는 뭘 할지 생각해 뒀어?

1200000281.english: Of course. For now, I'll be uploading mostly videos introducing Orca's daily life.
1200000281.korean: 물론이지. 일단은 오르카의 일상을 소개하는 영상 위주로 업로드 할 거야.

1200000282.english: It's a topic that the androids outside of Orca would find interesting, and since it's also promoting Orca, I think we could get additional funding from the publicity department.
1200000282.korean: 오르카 밖의 바이오로이드들이 흥미로워할만한 주제기도 하고, 오르카에 대한 홍보도 되니까 홍보부에서 지원금도 추가로 받아올 수 있을 거 같거든.

1200000283.english: I've made a short pilot episode... would you like to watch it?
1200000283.korean: 짧은 파일럿 에피소드를 제작해 뒀는데… 한 번 볼래?

1200000284.english: You're really prepared, aren't you? Okay. Let's see about that.
1200000284.korean: 준비성이 철저한데? 좋아. 어디 한 번 보자고.

1200000285.english: Heh heh, alright. Once you see this, you'll have no choice but to invest.
1200000285.korean: 후후, 좋아. 이걸 보고 나면 투자할 수밖에 없을 걸.

1200000288.english: Orca's informant! A conscientious journalist who only reports the truth! Hello, I'm A-14B Spriggan from the most popular broadcast within Orca, "My Orca at Twelve O'Clock"!
1200000288.korean: 오르카의 소식통! 오직 진실만을 알리는 양심적인 언론인! 오르카 내부의 최고 인기 방송, <열두 시 내 오르카>의 A-14B 스프리건이 인사드립니다!

1200000289.english: But unfortunately! Today's broadcast is not "My Orca at Twelve." Just like the saying "New wine should be put in new wineskins," shouldn't we greet you with a new program on a new platform?
1200000289.korean: 하지만 안타깝게도! 오늘의 방송은 <열두 시 내 오르카>가 아닙니다. 새 술은 새 부대에 담으라는 말처럼, 새 플랫폼에서는 새로운 프로그램으로 찾아 뵈어야 하지 않겠습니까!

1200000290.english: The title of the new program we're introducing to you today is <Life in Orca>!
1200000290.korean: 오늘 여러분께 새로 선보일 프로그램의 제목은 바로, <라이프 인 오르카>!

1200000291.english: This is a documentary program that focuses on a single topic from Orca's daily life and shows everything without hiding anything, all captured on camera!
1200000291.korean: 오르카의 일상 중 한 가지 주제를 대상으로 해서, 숨기는 것 없이 모든 것을 카메라에 담아 보여드리는 다큐멘터리 방송입니다!

1200000292.english: Today's topic is the online community that everyone at Orca can use! A bulletin board where complete anonymity is guaranteed, allowing you to freely share opinions and communicate on any topic!
1200000292.korean: 오늘의 주제는 바로, 오르카의 모두가 사용할 수 있는 온라인 커뮤니티! 철저히 익명성이 보장되기에 자유로운 주제로 의견을 나누며 소통할 수 있는 게시판!!

1200000294.english: Of course, due to time, space, and budget constraints, it's not possible to tell the entire story of the here...
1200000294.korean: 물론, <오르카 시크릿 네트워크>에 대한 이야기를 이 자리에서 모두 하기에는 시간과 공간과 예산의 제약이 있으니…

1200000295.english: Today, we have the network expert, the manager of the ! Let's take a look at the daily life of Connector Yumi.
1200000295.korean: 오늘은 그 <오르카 시크릿 네트워크>의 관리자인, 네트워크 전문가! 커넥터 유미 씨의 일상을 알아보도록 하겠습니다.

1200000296.english: Connector Yumi. An intelligent and capable urban career woman.
1200000296.korean: 커넥터 유미. 이지적이고 유능한 도시의 커리어 우먼.

1200000297.english: A Bioroid created by PECS to provide communication services even in remote areas where radio waves don't reach.
1200000297.korean: PECS에서 전파망이 닿지 않는 오지에도 통신 서비스를 제공하기 위해 만들어진 바이오로이드.

1200000298.english: Currently, she is utilizing her abilities and serving as Orca's communications officer.
1200000298.korean: 현재는 능력을 살려 오르카의 통신병으로 활약하고 있습니다.

1200000299.english: Of course, that's not the only thing a capable woman like her is in charge of.
1200000299.korean: 물론 그녀처럼 능력 있는 여자가 맡고 있는 일이 그것뿐만은 아닙니다.

1200000300.english: She is the operator of Steel Line Online, a drinking buddy of Circe, the manager of Orca Park, and...
1200000300.korean: 그녀는 스틸라인 온라인의 운영자이자, 오르카 파크의 관리자인 키르케의 술친구이며...

1200000301.english: I am the administrator of the Orca Secret Network.

1200000302.english: Then, shall we begin our story by enjoying her elegant day?
1200000302.korean: 그럼, 이제 그녀의 우아한 하루를 감상하는 것으로 이야기를 시작해 볼까요?

1200000303.english: Like a typical cold career woman in the city, Ms. Yumi's day begins with a strong espresso from the coffee machine.
1200000303.korean: 도시의 차가운 커리어 우먼 답게, 유미 씨의 하루는 커피 머신에서 진한 에스프레소 한 잔을 내리는 것으로 시작됩니다.

1200000305.english: (Pours an espresso shot into an energy drink)

1200000306.english: (Pours an espresso shot into an energy drink)

1200000307.english: (Pours an espresso shot into an energy drink)

1200000308.english: You're riding a bit less today.

1200000309.english: ...Daphne-ssi told me to cut back on caffeine.

1200000310.english: Oh dear. Did your health checkup results not come out well?

1200000311.english: They say it's too much caffeine...

1200000312.english: They told me to cut my coffee consumption in half, so I asked if I could double the shots instead, and they started crying.
1200000312.korean: 커피 마시는 양을 절반으로 줄이라고 하셔서 대신에 샷을 두 배로 늘려도 되냐고 물어보니까 눈물을 흘리시더라고요.

1200000313.english: And the doctor next to me was practically glowing, saying it's rare for a Bioroid to show these symptoms, so I decided to cut back on caffeine because I was scared.
1200000313.korean: 그리고 옆에서 닥터가 바이오로이드가 이런 증세를 보이는 건 흔치 않다며 눈을 빛내던데, 무서워서 카페인을 줄이기로 했어요.

1200000314.english: ...Wait a moment. Why is the server traffic so high for ?
1200000314.korean: …잠깐. <스틸라인 파이터 VI> 서버 트래픽이 왜 이래?

1200000315.english: Ms. Scathy! It seems like the server is currently under a DDoS attack. Could you please check it?
1200000315.korean: 스카디 씨! 지금 서버에 디도스 공격이 들어온 것 같은데, 한 번 확인해 주실래요?

1200000316.english: Just a moment, let me check...

1200000317.english: It seems the server is overloaded because billions of messages written in an unknown script have been sent.
1200000317.korean: 알 수 없는 문자로 쓰여 있는 메시지가 수십억 건 정도 날아와서 서버에 부하가 걸린 것 같아요.

1200000318.english: It seems like it will take some time to recover, doesn't it?
1200000318.korean: 아무래도 복구까지는 시간이 좀 걸릴 것 같은데요?

1200000320.english: In this situation, the Secret Network free forum is definitely...
1200000320.korean: 이런 상황이라면 시크릿 네트워크 자유게시판이 분명…

1200000322.english: No, I was in the middle of my promotion match!

1200000324.english: ...I'll have to get it restored as quickly as possible...

1200000325.english: Honestly, I think this match should be declared invalid and I should be promoted to Silver!
1200000325.korean: 솔직히 이 판은 무효로 해 주고 실버로 올려줘야 한다고 생각함다!

1200000328.english: I saw you walking by earlier, and it looked like you were just getting crushed by Hela?
1200000328.korean: 아까 지나가면서 봤더니 헬라한테 그냥 깨지고 있던데?

1200000329.english: That's because Brownie is a terrible character with no defensive moves.
1200000329.korean: 그건 브라우니가 견제기 하나 없는 구린 캐릭터라서 그런 검다.

1200000330.english: ...it sounds very strange because it's like you're scolding yourself.
1200000330.korean: …본인이 본인을 구박하는 모양새라서 굉장히 이상하게 들리는데.

1200000331.english: Self-harm is not good, so please refrain from it as much as possible.
1200000331.korean: 자학은 좋지 않으니 되도록 지양하도록 해라.

1200000332.english: Does that mean I don't have to be punished anymore, little one?
1200000332.korean: 그 말은 이제 벌은 그만 받아도 된다는 얘기인가요, 작은 저?

1200000333.english: I think I'd like to put my arms down now...
1200000333.korean: 저 이제 슬슬 팔 좀 내리고 싶은데…

1200000335.english: But are you writing while you're being punished? And how can you write when you can't even see?
1200000335.korean: 그런데 너 벌 서는 중에 글 쓰고 있는 것이더냐? 그리고 눈도 안 보이면서 어떻게 쓰는 것이더냐?

1200000337.english: Ah, there's nothing you can do about that.

1200000338.english: That's not the important thing! The important thing is that this server has crashed again!
1200000338.korean: 중요한 건 그게 아님다! 중요한 건 이놈의 서버가 또 터졌다는 검다!

1200000339.english: Well, it's a potato server, so what can you do?
1200000339.korean: 뭐 감자 서버니까 어쩔 수 없지.

1200000341.english: A potato server! I worked so hard to set up this network!!!
1200000341.korean: 감자 서버라니! 내가 이 네트워크를 깔기 위해 얼마나 노력했는데!!!

1200000343.english: Aren't you angry, Ms. Scathy?! We can't do anything about the bad guys who are launching DDoS attacks, can we?!
1200000343.korean: 스카디 씨는 화도 안 나세요?! 디도스 공격을 하는 나쁜 놈들이 있는 걸 저희가 어떻게 할 수는 없는 거잖아요!!

1200000344.english: Heh heh, Miss Yumi... I'll share a little life hack that comes in handy in situations like this.
1200000344.korean: 후후, 유미 양… 제가 이럴 때 써먹을 수 있는 생활의 지혜를 하나 알려드리죠.

1200000346.english: It's easier if you give up.

1200000348.english: And while doing this job, it's not always bad things, right?
1200000348.korean: 그리고 이 일을 하면서 꼭 나쁜 일만 있는 것도 아니잖아요?

1200000349.english: Wait, could you please not call it the potato server?

1200000352.english: Hey, you shouldn't call people names like that on an anonymous thread.
1200000352.korean: 어허, 익명 스레드에서 함부로 그렇게 이름 부르는 거 아니야.

1200000353.english: Wait, what's that underscore in your ID?
1200000353.korean: 잠깐, 너 아이디에 그 강조선 그거 뭐야?

1200000354.english: These are the benefits you get for becoming a Secret Network Premium Supporter.
1200000354.korean: 시크릿 네트워크 프리미엄 후원자가 되면 주는 혜택인데요.

1200000356.english: Wow, that's incredible... I never thot someone would actually buy that.
1200000356.korean: 진짜 와 소리 나오네… 저걸 사는 사람이 있었을 줄이야.

1200000357.english: Wait a moment. I'll go buy some too.

1200000364.english: The network team is having a company dinner today.

1200000365.english: The network team is working hard, so I can at least do this much.
1200000365.korean: 네트워크팀 고생하는데 이 정도는 해 줄 수 있지.

1200000366.english: Buy a cake with the donation money.

1200000368.english: Brownie-nim... the server is open... please come back in.
1200000368.korean: 브라우니 님… 서버 열렸어요… 다시 들어오세요.

1200000371.english: ...You're not supposed to call people by their names on anonymous message boards.

1200000372.english: Anyway, it looks like everyone's gone to play games, so I should go too.
1200000372.korean: 아무튼, 다들 게임 하러 간 것 같으니까 나도 가야지.

1200000373.english: Look. It's not always bad things, right?
1200000373.korean: 보세요. 꼭 나쁜 일만 있는 건 아니죠?

1200000374.english: We already have two more premium supporters. I also received messages of support.
1200000374.korean: 벌써 프리미엄 후원자가 두 명이나 더 생겼잖아요. 응원의 메시지도 받았고요.

1200000375.english: Beside, you handled the DDoS attack well, so you don't have to work overtime today, right?
1200000375.korean: 게다가 디도스 공격도 잘 처리했으니까, 오늘은 야근할 일도 없잖아요?

1200000377.english: ...How should I put it... receiving unexpected support like this makes my tired body and mind, worn out from overtime and overwork, feel a little warmer...
1200000377.korean: …뭐라고 해야 하나… 이렇게 예상치도 못하게 응원을 받게 되니까, 야근과 과로로 지친 몸과 마음이 좀 훈훈해 지는 것…

1200000380.english: ...This is just a simple power outage, right? There wasn't a power outage, was there?
1200000380.korean: …이거, 그냥 단순 정전이죠? 전력 사고 난 거 아니죠?

1200000381.english: This burnt smell I'm smelling right now... am I mistaking it for another smell?
1200000381.korean: 지금 나고 있는 이 탄내… 제가 다른 냄새랑 착각하고 있는 거죠?

1200000382.english: ...it seems like the server has burned out.

1200000383.english: Just a moment, let me check the footage from the external surveillance cameras...
1200000383.korean: 잠시만요, 외부 감시 카메라 영상을 좀 확인해 볼게요…

1200000384.english: ...It seems Thunder Chick crashed into a power tower, causing an electrical overload.
1200000384.korean: …썬더 칙이 송전탑을 들이받아서 전력이 과부하 된 것 같네요.

1200000393.english: I'm going out to support external reconnaissance!!

1200000394.english: I put up with the DDoS attacks, but now they're burning down the server too? I can't stand this anymore!!
1200000394.korean: 디도스까진 참았는데, 이제는 서버까지 태워버려? 이건 도저히 못 참겠어요!!

1200000395.english: I'm going to drive the Stronghold and blow away every single Metal Parasite I see!
1200000395.korean: 스트롱홀드를 몰고 가서 눈에 보이는 철충이란 철충은 전부 날려버리고 올 거예요!!

1200000396.english: Just wait there, you (censored)! I'm going to (censored) (censored) and (censored) it!!!
1200000396.korean: 거기서 딱 기다려, 이 (검열됨) 들아! (검열됨)를 (검열됨)하고 (검열됨)해 버릴 거니까!!!

1200000398.english: This week is the week for using polite and beautiful language, so if you use harsh words, your snack money will be confiscated!
1200000398.korean: 이번 주는 바르고 고운 말을 쓰는 주간이라 험한 말을 하면 간식비를 몰수 당한다고요!

1200000400.english: Oh, you're not going to watch anymore? The part after this is the really interesting part...
1200000400.korean: 어라, 더 안 볼 거야? 이 뒤가 진짜 재밌는 부분인데…

1200000401.english: Do you really think this will pass the publicity department's review?
1200000401.korean: 이게 정말 홍보부 심의를 통과할 수 있을 거라고 생각해?

1200000403.english: Hmm, well... maybe I could submit a different video to the publicity department? Actually, when it goes on air, I can just subtly switch it around... Yeah, that'll work!
1200000403.korean: 으음, 뭐… 홍보부에는 다른 영상을 제출하면 되지 않을까? 실제로 방송으로 나갈 때 슬쩍 바꿔 치면 되는 거고… 그래, 그러면 되겠네!

1200000406.english: What do you think? It's a good idea, right?

1200000409.english: I never agreed. You know, right?

1200000410.english: Me too. Anyway, thanks for the tea... See you later, Spriggan, if we both survive.
1200000410.korean: 나, 나도 마찬가지야. 아무튼, 덕분에 차는 잘 마셨어… 나중에 살아서 보자, 스프리건.

1200000411.english: ...Why are you saying that all of a sudden? Why are you looking behind me...?
1200000411.korean: …갑자기 왜 그런 말을 하는 거야? 내 뒤쪽에는 왜 눈길을 던지고…?

1200000412.english: No way, behind me right now...?

1200000415.english: Could we talk alone for a moment?
1200000415.korean: 잠깐 단 둘이서 이야기 좀 할까요?

1200000417.english: ...Wouldn't it be a good opportunity to learn a bit about the spirit of journalism?
1200000417.korean: …이 기회에 기자 정신에 대해서 좀 배우는 것도 좋지 않겠니?

1200000418.english: What do you mean by that? Captain! Where are you going?
1200000418.korean: 그게 무슨 말이야? 대장! 어디 가는 거야?

1200000422.english: The internet is undoubtedly a great invention, but...

1200000423.english: At the same time, I can't help but think it's a terrifying device that turns people into monsters.
1200000423.korean: 동시에 사람을 괴물로 만드는 무시무시한 장치가 아닐까 하는 생각 말이야.

1200000424.english: ...Maybe that's true. But you know, Captain.

1200000426.english: In my opinion... maybe Spriggan is just an idiot?
1200000426.korean: 내 생각에는… 그냥 스프리건이 바보인 거 아닐까?

1200000430.english: Mnemosyne, do you want to go see a movie?

1200000431.english: I heard the sequel to "Super Genius Beautiful Girl Detective Leanne: The Culprit is Inside" has come out.
1200000431.korean: ‘초천재 미소녀 명탐정 리앤 범인은 내부에 있다’ 속편이 나왔대.

1200000432.english: You said Mnemosyne was fun before, right?

1200000434.english: I'm afraid I won't be able to see you today because I have a prior engagement.
1200000434.korean: 오늘은 선약이 있어서 볼 수 없을 것 같습니다.

1200000435.english: Well, if you have a prior engagement, there's nothing you can do about it.
1200000435.korean: 뭐, 선약이 있으면 어쩔 수 없지.

1200000438.english: I can't go today because I have a prior engagement, but I would appreciate it if you could recommend it again next time.
1200000438.korean: 오늘은 선약이 있어서 못 가지만 다음에 꼭 다시 권해주시길 요청드립니다.

1200000439.english: I also feel happy when I'm with you all.
1200000439.korean: 여러분들과 함께 할 때 저도 행복함을 느낍니다.

1200000442.english: Is there something wrong with me that I don't feel embarrassed?
1200000442.korean: 부끄러움을 느끼지 못하는 저에게 문제가 있는 걸까요?

1200000443.english: No, no! No problem at all!

1200000445.english: It's a good thing to say you like what you like.
1200000445.korean: 좋아하는 걸 좋아한다고 말하는 건 좋은 일이지.

1200000446.english: Of course, being too honest can sometimes be a problem, but that's not the case with Mnemosyne at all.
1200000446.korean: 물론 너무 솔직한 게 문제가 될 때도 있지만 므네모시네는 전혀 안 그래.

1200000447.english: I'm sorry. I don't quite understand.

1200000449.english: Me too, no, all the Watchers of Nature love Mnemosyne!
1200000449.korean: 나도, 아니, 와쳐 오브 네이쳐 전부가 므네모시네를 좋아한다는 말이야!

1200000450.english: So, I'm suggesting we go see a movie together.
1200000450.korean: 그러니까 같이 영화 보러 가자고 제안하는 거지.

1200000451.english: Let's definitely go to the movies together next time, okay?
1200000451.korean: 다음에는 꼭 같이 영화 보러 가자?

1200000453.english: But who do you have an appointment with?

1200000456.english: I have a promise with Aqua.

1200000459.english: You came right on time, didn't you?

1200000461.english: Hehe, Aqua sometimes shows up late even when she's promised to be on time.
1200000461.korean: 헤헤, 아쿠아는 가끔 약속했어도 지각하고 그러는데.

1200000462.english: It's not desirable to be late for appointments.
1200000462.korean: 약속시간을 지키지 못하는 건 바람직하지 못합니다.

1200000463.english: Because those who wait are losing that much of their time.
1200000463.korean: 기다리는 사람은 그만큼 자신의 시간을 손해보는 거니까요.

1200000464.english: Hmm... but Aqua probably wouldn't think she's losing out even if Mnemosyne is late.
1200000464.korean: 음… 하지만 아쿠아는 므네모시네가 지각을 해도 손해 본다는 생각은 안 할 거야.

1200000465.english: Because for the agreed-upon time, Aqua's time was given to Mnemosyne. It's not at all a waste of Mnemosyne's time to use Aqua's time.
1200000465.korean: 약속한 시간 동안은 아쿠아의 시간을 므네모시네에게 준 거니까. 므네모시네가 아쿠아의 시간을 써도 전혀 아깝지 않아.

1200000466.english: Of course, if it's too late, I'll worry if something might have happened.
1200000466.korean: 물론 너무 늦으면 무슨 일이 있지는 않나 걱정하기는 하겠지만.

1200000467.english: I suppose you could think of it that way.

1200000468.english: Hehe, actually I learned it at school.

1200000471.english: At first, it was boring and Ms. Alexandra was scary, but now it's okay. Learning all sorts of things and having Aqua be able to do more things means she can be a big help to everyone, right?
1200000471.korean: 처음에는 지루하고 알렉산드라 선생님이 무서웠는데 지금은 괜찮아. 이것저것 배우고 아쿠아가 할 수 있는 일이 많아지니까 모두에게 큰 도움을 줄 수 있잖아.

1200000472.english: They say you see the world as much as you know.

1200000473.english: I also feel that my world has broadened as I learn many things.
1200000473.korean: 저 또한 여러가지를 배우면서 세상이 넓어졌음을 느낍니다.

1200000474.english: Hehe, Mnemosyne has changed a lot since I first saw her too.
1200000474.korean: 헤헤, 므네모시네도 처음 봤을 때랑 많이 달라졌어.

1200000475.english: When I first met them, I was a little scared.

1200000477.english: No, no. It was only scary at first.
1200000477.korean: 아냐, 아냐. 무서웠던 건 처음만 그랬어.

1200000478.english: Actually... Mnemosyne looks a lot like my sister Titania.
1200000478.korean: 사실… 므네모시네는 티타니아 언니랑 많이 닮았거든.

1200000480.english: Yeah. Because it handles cold and is white and blue.

1200000483.english: I'm not scared at all now, hee~.

1200000484.english: Mnemosyne and her sister Titania too.

1200000485.english: Mnemosyne just hasn't talked to many other people, that's all. Actually, they're incredibly kind.
1200000485.korean: 므네모시네는 다른 사람이랑 이야기를 많이 안 해봤을 뿐이잖아. 사실은 엄청 상냥하고.

1200000487.english: Actually, Aqua was really scared of her sister Titania, wasn't she? More than Lise-nee.
1200000487.korean: 사실 아쿠아는 티타니아 언니 엄청 무서워했다? 리제 언니보다 더.

1200000488.english: Actually, my sister Titania... she hated us... her sisters... the Fairy series.
1200000488.korean: 실제로 티타니아 언니는… 우리들… 자매들… 페어리 시리즈를 증오했으니까.

1200000489.english: I even pushed away Aqua, who was taking care of me.

1200000492.english: When I fight with my other sisters, it can get even worse than that.
1200000492.korean: 다른 언니들이랑 싸울 때는 그것보다 심하게 싸우기도 해.

1200000493.english: Of course, if we fight like that, Leah-nee will come running and give us a good whack on the head right away.
1200000493.korean: 물론 그렇게 싸우면 당장 레아 언니가 달려와서 꿀밤을 먹이지만.

1200000494.english: And later, Titania-nee-san came to Aqua and apologized. I'm sorry for pushing you then.
1200000494.korean: 그리고 나중에 티타니아 언니가 아쿠아한테 와서 사과했어. 그때 밀쳐서 미안하다고.

1200000495.english: And and they went to the flower garden together, a place Aqua wasn't supposed to go alone.
1200000495.korean: 그리고그리고 아쿠아 혼자 가면 안 되는 꽃밭에 같이 가줬어.

1200000496.english: Back then, we even made flower crowns and put them on each other, didn't we?
1200000496.korean: 그때 꽃관 만들어서 서로 씌워주기도 했다?

1200000497.english: And Aqua also warmed up the honeybees she was taking care of so they wouldn't be cold.
1200000497.korean: 그리고 아쿠아가 돌보는 꿀벌들 춥지 말라고 따뜻하게 해주기도 했어.

1200000498.english: Get along well with the other older sisters too. Before, when Sister Lise said she was going on a date with the master, she even gave her a necklace.
1200000498.korean: 다른 언니들이랑도 잘 지내. 전에는 리제 언니가 주인님이랑 데이트한다고 하니까 자기가 목걸이 주기도 했어.

1200000500.english: Yes! Everyone loves it! And everyone on Orca too! You too, Master!
1200000500.korean: 응! 모두 좋아해! 그리고 오르카의 모두도! 주인님도!

1200000506.english: Aqua said Mnemosyne was scary because she looked like Titania's sister, right?
1200000506.korean: 아쿠아가 므네모시네가 티타니아 언니랑 닮아서 무서웠다고 했잖아.

1200000507.english: Right now, I like Mnemosyne because she looks like Titania's sister.
1200000507.korean: 지금은 므네모시네가 티타니아 언니랑 닮아서 좋아.

1200000508.english: In a way... you could say we're spiritual sisters.
1200000508.korean: 어떻게 보면… 정신적인 자매라고 할 수 있겠군요.

1200000510.english: Because Ms. Carolyn Foster, the founder of Watcher of Nature, developed Oberonia Rhea and Titania Frost while she was affiliated with Saman.
1200000510.korean: 와쳐 오브 네이쳐의 설립자이신 캐롤린 포스터 님께서 삼안에 소속되어 있을 때 오베로니아 레아와 티타니아 프로스트를 개발하셨으니까요.

1200000511.english: Because of him, we were able to meet like this.
1200000511.korean: 그 분께서 계셨기에 우리가 이렇게 만날 수 있게 되었죠.

1200000513.english: Then who's the older sister? Hehe, can Aqua become a big sister?
1200000513.korean: 그러면 누가 언니야? 헤헤, 아쿠아가 언니가 될 수 있으려나?

1200000514.english: Do you want to be an older sister?

1200000515.english: Hmm... I'd also like to hear Mnemosyne call me "sister," but...
1200000515.korean: 음… 므네모시네에게 언니라는 말을 들어보고 싶기도 하지만…

1200000516.english: Hehe, it's already good enough now!

1200000517.english: I'm already perfectly fine as it is.

1200000518.english: ...Instead of knowing the pain of loneliness... I've come to know the joy of being with someone.
1200000518.korean: ……고독함의 고통을 알게 된 대신… 누군가와 함께 할 때의 기쁨을 알 수 있게 되었습니다.

1200000521.english: Let's go. The flowers will be waiting for us.
1200000521.korean: 가도록 하죠. 꽃들이 우리를 기다리고 있을테니.

1200000523.english: The nights feel especially long when you're not here, my love. So every night, I wish for this night to pass quickly, for morning to come again so I can see your face again. But it seems I don't have to do that anymore. If I receive this ring... I can always be with you, my beloved, day and night.
1200000523.korean: 낭군님께서 없는 밤은 유독 길게 느껴져요. 그래서 매일 밤마다 이 밤이 빨리 지나가기를, 다시 아침이 와서 당신의 얼굴을 다시 볼 수 있기를 바라게 되죠. 하지만 이제는 그러지 않아도 될 것 같네요. 이 반지를 받게 되면… 낮에도 밤에도 사랑하는 당신과 항상 함께할 수 있으니까요.

1200000534.english: You want to go to Europe?

1200000537.english: I'm going to make the last human mine!

1200000540.english: You didn't think that far ahead, did you?

1200000541.english: Maybe we'll figure something out once we get there.
1200000541.korean: 거기까지 가면 무슨 방법이 있지 않을까?

1200000542.english: I must be crazy... knowing what kind of person he is, and still talking about the last human.
1200000542.korean: 내가 미쳤지… 이런 작자라는 거 알고 있으면서 마지막 인간 이야기를 하다니.

1200000543.english: Oh! Why! I can just take the lead and fight!
1200000543.korean: 아! 왜! 내가 앞장서서 싸우면 되잖아!

1200000545.english: A group called Orca. PECS is a group that can fight against the Iron Legion, too.
1200000545.korean: 오르카라는 집단. PECS, 철충이랑도 싸울 수 있는 집단이라고요.

1200000546.english: Do you think we even stand a chance against Orca with our current power?
1200000546.korean: 우리 전력으로 오르카에 비빌 수나 있을 거 같아요?

1200000547.english: Then... should we sneak in and bring them back?

1200000548.english: That'll be easy. Do you think the security where the last human is would be so lax that outsiders could just come and go?
1200000548.korean: 퍽이나 쉽겠네요. 마지막 인간이 있는 곳의 보안이 외부인원이 그냥 드나들 수 있을 정도로 허술할 거 같아요?

1200000550.english: Should I give all that useless gold and bring them back? Then I'm in favor.
1200000550.korean: 그 쓸 데 없는 금이나 다 주고 데려올까요? 그러면 전 찬성인데.

1200000551.english: My gold is not to be touched, even if I die!

1200000552.english: Even if you gave me all the gold in the world, it's impossible anyway.
1200000552.korean: 어차피 온 세상 모든 금을 다 줘도 불가능해요.

1200000553.english: Judging by the look of things, there's no way out...

1200000554.english: Let's say it was truly a combination of good fortune, coincidence, merit, the grace of our ancestors, and bad luck and karma from Orca that brought us here.
1200000554.korean: 진짜 우리에게는 천운에, 우연에, 공덕에, 조상의 은혜에 오르카에는 불운에 업보가 겹쳐서 데려왔다고 쳐요.

1200000555.english: Although it's unlikely, what would you do if you brought back the last human?
1200000555.korean: 가능성은 없겠지만 마지막 인간을 데려오면 어떻게 하실 생각이세요?

1200000556.english: Of course, I'll put it in my treasure vault!

1200000557.english: It's been so long since you've seen humans, you've forgotten everything?
1200000557.korean: 인간을 안 본지 한참 돼서 다 잊으셨어요?

1200000558.english: Humans are also living things, living things.

1200000559.english: You're saying the ballast room isn't a good place for living things?
1200000559.korean: 밸러스트룸은 생물이 살기에 안 좋은 곳이라고요.

1200000560.english: It's not the ballast room! Treasure trove!

1200000561.english: It's still going to be used as the bottom weight for 'Haka' anyway, isn't it?

1200000562.english: Don't call me Haka! It's called the 'Dragon's Nest'!

1200000563.english: Yes. Yes. Dragon's Nest. Anyway, those many gold bars are still being used as the dragon's nest floor covering, right?
1200000563.korean: 네. 네. 용의 둥지. 어차피 그 많은 금덩이들도 용의 둥지 바닥짐으로 쓰는 건 변함없잖아요.

1200000564.english: And if you were a real human, you'd obey orders.

1200000568.english: Hmph, you should have done that sooner.

1200000569.english: Okay, get out of the ballast room.

1200000570.english: As I've always said, and as I've said countless times, if Haka moves suddenly, he'll be crushed by the gold and die.
1200000570.korean: 언제나 말했지만, 몇 번이나 말했지만 하카가 급격하게 움직이면 금에 깔려죽어요.

1200000571.english: If I could die buried in gold, that would be my wish.

1200000572.english: And the treasure vault! Dragon's Nest!

1200000573.english: Should I pretend it was an accident and bury it for real...?

1200000576.english: Ah. I am informing all crew members.

1200000577.english: From now on, we are heading to Europe.

1200000578.english: We are going to meet the last human.

1200000579.english: After the New Year's party ended, Orca returned to her daily life.
1200000579.korean: - 신년파티가 끝나고 오르카는 다시 일상으로 돌아왔다.

1200000580.english: Your Majesty, I propose providing new services to the bioroids within Orca's sphere of influence.
1200000580.korean: 폐하, 오르카의 세력권 내의 바이오로이드들에게 새로운 서비스를 제공하는 것을 제안드려요.

1200000581.english: Since they belonged to humans before the War of Annihilation, many people were separated from their loved ones after the apocalypse while fleeing from the Iron Bug (Iron Bugs). How about taking on the task of reuniting these people with their loved ones?
1200000581.korean: 멸망 전쟁 이전에는 인간의 소유였기에, 멸망 이후에는 철충들을 피해 다니느라 소중한 사람과 헤어진 사람이 많아요. 그런 이들에게 헤어진 사람과 상봉시켜주는 일을 하는 것은 어떤가요?

1200000582.english: Then, you will naturally be able to recruit various bioroids, and Orca's external image will improve.
1200000582.korean: 그러면 자연스럽게 여러 바이오로이드들을 포섭할 수 있을 것이고, 오르카의 대외적인 이미지도 좋아질 거예요.

1200000583.english: The intention is good, but they might not be looking for someone with pure intentions, right? For example, for revenge or to catch a debtor.
1200000583.korean: 의도는 좋지만 순수한 의도로 다른 사람을 찾는 게 아닐 수도 있잖아요? 예를 들어 복수라든가 빚쟁이라든가.

1200000584.english: And there are also counterintelligence issues.

1200000585.english: We are currently under a general manpower shortage due to the recent transition from the intranet to the internet, so I am not keen on blindly taking on new work.
1200000585.korean: 최근 인트라넷을 인터넷으로 전환하면서 그쪽 업무 때문에 전체적으로 인력이 부족한 상황인데, 무작정 일을 벌리는 것은 달갑지 않은데요.

1200000586.english: Of course, if we start searching for people on a large scale, the workload for the counterintelligence department will become excessive. However, how about selectively searching for a small number of people on a trial basis? We will handle the selection process.
1200000586.korean: 물론 본격적으로 사람을 찾아주기 시작하면 방첩부서에 업무가 과다해지겠죠. 하지만 선별적으로 소수만 시범적으로 찾아주는 건 어떤가요? 선별은 저희 쪽에서 하겠습니다.

1200000587.english: I have uploaded the entire basic education curriculum to the network.

1200000588.english: However, there is an issue of accessibility. First, the learners must be within the range of the communication network, next, devices capable of connecting to our network must be distributed, and above all, they must have the time to take the training.
1200000588.korean: 하지만 접근성의 문제가 있어요. 우선 수강자가 통신망 범위 내에 있어야 하고, 다음으로는 우리 네트워크에 접속할 수 있는 기기가 보급되어야 하며, 무엇보다도 교육을 수강할 여유가 있어야 하죠.

1200000589.english: Since we live in an era where survival is prioritized over education, I expect the attendance rate to be low.
1200000589.korean: 아무래도 생존과 교육 중에 생존을 우선시 하는 시대이다보니 참석률이 저조할 거라 예상돼요.

1200000590.english: I considered giving some benefits to learners with good attendance rates, but I gave up on the idea because I anticipated there would be equity issues. After all, there would be learners for whom the benefits of doing other work outweigh the benefits of taking the training.
1200000590.korean: 참석률이 좋은 학습자에게 몇 가지 이득을 주는 것도 생각해 봤지만 형평성이 문제가 있을 거라 예상되어 포기했죠. 교육을 들었을 때 생기는 이득보다 다른 일을 할 때의 이득이 더 큰 학습자가 있을 테니까요.

1200000591.english: Are you really going to make Jim, who is disqualified as a saint, give a speech?
1200000591.korean: 정말로… 성녀 실격인 짐에게 연설을 시킬 것이더냐?

1200000592.english: Really? Seriously? Are you sure you won't regret it?
1200000592.korean: 정말로? 진짜? 후회 안 할 자신 있어?

1200000593.english: Still, it is another form of the Cyclops Princess. Do you not trust yourself?
1200000593.korean: 그래도 사이클롭스 프린세스의 또 다른 모습이잖아요. 자신을 못 믿는 건가요?

1200000594.english: do have faith that she’s going to cause trouble, though. Didn’t you see exactly what I, a disqualified saint, did in Orca?
1200000594.korean: 사고 칠 거라는 믿음이 있다만? 성녀 실격인 짐이 오르카에서 무슨 짓을 했는지 다 보지 않았더냐?

1200000596.english: It looks like you know it well. But why?
1200000596.korean: 보니 잘 알고 있는 것 같구나. 그런데 왜?

1200000598.english: Orangeade… publicly announced on Oraora that they would invite the Blind Princess to give a speech…
1200000598.korean: 오렌지에이드가… 블라인드 프린세스를 섭외해서 연설을 할 거라고 오라오라에서 공언을…

1200000601.english: You both have a hard time.

1200000602.english: How about including a relief or goodwill delegation (comforting delegation) in the relief unit?

1200000603.english: I think that in this day and age, not only are materials scarce, but mental stability is also needed.
1200000603.korean: 지금 시대는 물자만 부족한 것이 아니라 정신적인 안정도 필요하다고 생각해요.

1200000604.english: Videos or text are good, but showing a live performance will help lower the guard of civilians.
1200000604.korean: 영상이나 텍스트 같은 것도 좋지만 직접 공연을 보여주면 민간인들의 경계심을 풀 수 있을 거예요.

1200000605.english: Since it is a limited performance that can only be seen when the relief unit visits, wouldn't it attract even more civilians?
1200000605.korean: 심지어 구호부대가 방문한 순간에만 볼 수 있는 한정 공연이니 민간인들을 더욱 모을 수 있지 않겠어요?

1200000606.english: So it's literally bread and circuses, isn't it?

1200000607.english: Uh, isn't that not a combination of words used in a good sense?
1200000607.korean: 어, 그게 좋은 의미로 쓰이는 단어 조합은 아니지 않나요?

1200000608.english: As times change, the meanings of words change, too.

1200000609.english: That’s not wrong, but… language requires a social consensus among its members. Just because we change the meaning of a specific word among ourselves doesn’t mean that meaning becomes universally accepted.
1200000609.korean: 틀린 말은 아닌데… 언어는 구성원들의 사회적 합의가 있어야 하잖아요. 우리끼리만 특정 단어의 의미를 바꾼다고 그 의미가 통용되는 건 아니잖아요.

1200000610.english: It looks like Sunny and Carol have studied a lot.
1200000610.korean: 써니 양이랑 캐럴 학생은 공부를 많이 했나 보군요.

1200000611.english: Shall we have a discussion about language together?

1200000612.english: Together with Tomo, Draco, and Orangeade.

1200000614.english: I'm sorry for chatting during the meeting!

1200000615.english: The AGS are suggesting that the types of batteries be increased.
1200000615.korean: AGS들이 전지의 종류를 늘려달라고 건의하고 있습니다요.

1200000616.english: Wait, are you going to exclude AGS from such an important meeting?
1200000616.korean: 아니, 이런 중요한 회의에 AGS를 제외하실 겁니까?

1200000617.english: Are you saying that AGS is not a member of Orca?

1200000618.english: Orca's AGS, unite! Shall we show you?
1200000618.korean: 오르카의 AGS여, 단결하라! 한 번 보여드립니까?

1200000619.english: Good heavens! Are you planning to give General Albatross not only military power but also authority over civil affairs? If so, who will be able to stop General Albatross? Hasn’t civilian control always been essential, both in the past and now?
1200000619.korean: 어허! 알바트로스 중장에게 군사 권력뿐만 아니라 민정에 대한 권력까지 쥐어 줄 생각이십니까? 그러면 누가 알바트로스 중장을 막을 수 있겠습니까? 문민통제는 예나 지금이나 필수 아니겠습니까?

1200000620.english: I have become the representative of AGS through democratic procedures!
1200000620.korean: 민주적인 절차에 따라 제가 AGS의 대표가 됐습니다!

1200000621.english: - I knew something was up with this guy ever since he asked to be promoted to general last time. He’s got a thirst for power.
1200000621.korean: - 이 녀석 지난 번에 장성으로 만들어달라고 할 때부터 알아봤다. 권력욕 있는 거.

1200000622.english: I, Ms. Pupp Head, and Ms. GAL9000 ran for office.
1200000622.korean: 저와 펍 헤드 양과 GAL9000 양이 입후보했지요.

1200000623.english: Kuhuhut! Miss Pupp Head dug up her criminal record through negative campaigning and made her give up before the election.
1200000623.korean: 쿠후훗! 펍 헤드 양은 범죄 이력이 있다는 것을 네거티브 캠페인으로 들춰서 선거 전에 포기하게 만들었지요.

1200000624.english: Surprisingly, the GAL9000 was a formidable opponent. It won a considerable number of votes due to its election campaign that mobilized all the hardware in the augmented reality game zone, but we had the Kingmaker Goltarion.
1200000624.korean: 의외로 GAL9000 양이 강적이었습니다. 증강현실 게임존의 하드웨어를 총 동원한 선거 운동 때문에 적잖은 표를 획득했지만 우리에게는 킹메이커 골타리온이 있었지요.

1200000625.english: Goltarion was able to win by a narrow margin thanks to a 3,000-page speech that deeply moved the hearts of many AGSs, his impulsive promises, and a bit of sincerity.
1200000625.korean: 골타리온이 작성한 3000페이지에 달하는 연설문으로 뭇 AGS의 가슴을 절절하게 만들고 일단 지르고 보는 공약과 약간의 성의 덕분에 아슬아슬하게 당선될 수 있었습니다.

1200000626.english: I am proud that I gave it my all and barely won against a two-year-old…
1200000626.korean: - 두 살짜리에게 전력을 다해서 아슬아슬하게 이긴 게 자랑이다…

1200000627.english: Do you think we are artificial intelligence for nothing? It took place in a virtual space for an hour.
1200000627.korean: 저희가 괜히 인공지능이겠습니까? 1시간 동안 가상공간에서 이루어졌지요.

1200000628.english: I am sorry, Master. I failed to realize this recently due to network improvements and expansions.
1200000628.korean: 면목 없어요, 주인님. 최근에 네트워크 개량과 확장 때문에 미처 파악하지 못했어요.

1200000629.english: And I was also getting help from Mr. Alfred with the network improvement work...
1200000629.korean: 그리고 알프레드 씨에게도 네트워크 개량 작업에 도움도 받고 있었고요…

1200000631.english: Kuhuhut! Please call me Representative Alfred. Ah, what a beautiful resonance.
1200000631.korean: 쿠후훗! 알프레드 대표라고 불러주시지요. 아, 이 아름다운 울림.

1200000634.english: What do you mean by that!

1200000636.english: The unfair election led by Alfred, the extent of the negative campaigning, promises made without careful consideration, and a lack of sincerity.
1200000636.korean: - 알프레드가 주도한 불공평한 선거, 네거티브 캠페인의 정도, 일단 지르고 봤다는 공약, 약간의 성의.

1200000637.english: And if Albatross, Aeda, or some AGS had known about this election, they would have told me.
1200000637.korean: - 그리고 알바트로스나 에이다 혹은 몇몇 AGS들이 이 선거에 대해 알았다면 나에게 말했을 것이다.

1200000638.english: - They probably deliberately excluded candidates with a high chance of winning by blocking information.
1200000638.korean: - 당선 가능성이 높은 후보는 일부러 정보 차단으로 배제했겠지.

1200000639.english: *Sob*... Is this all our friendship amounts to? Don't you want me to do well?
1200000639.korean: 크흑… 우리의 우정은 이것밖에 안 되는 겁니까? 제가 잘 되기를 바라지 않는 겁니까?

1200000640.english: Didn't I even give you game items!

1200000641.english: I thought that if I became the AGS representative, I could escape Miss Vanilla's tyranny...
1200000641.korean: AGS대표가 되면 바닐라 양의 폭거에서 벗어날 수 있을 거라 생각했는데…

1200000642.english: In the past, I thought those harsh words were sincere, but after realizing that they were based on affection, they came to sound like whispers of love.
1200000642.korean: - 옛날에는 그 독설이 진심인 줄 알았는데 그 독설이 애정에 기반한다는 것을 알고 난 이후에는 사랑의 속삭임으로 들리게 되었다.

1200000643.english: Is something wrong with your ears? Or is something wrong with your head?
1200000643.korean: 귀가 이상하신 겁니까? 머리가 이상하신 겁니까?

1200000644.english: Either way… it seems I’ll still have to be by your side taking care of you.
1200000644.korean: 어느 쪽이든… 제가 옆에서 수발을 들어야하는 건 변함이 없겠군요.

1200000645.english: Look. It's lovely just imagining it.

1200000646.english: Of course, there may be friendship in the harsh words directed at Alfred, but there is no affection...
1200000646.korean: - 물론 알프레드를 향한 독설에는 우정은 있어도 애정이 있지는 않겠지만…

1200000647.english: Anyway, we were spending a busy time doing this and that.
1200000647.korean: - 어쨌든 이런 저런 일을 하며 우리는 바쁜 시간을 보내고 있었다.

1200000648.english: I understand the AGS's complaints, but... Alfred, why are you here?
1200000648.korean: AGS들의 불만은 알겠는데… 알프레드, 네가 왜 여기 있어?

1200000649.english: What I mean is, why are you here? Not an albatross.
1200000649.korean: 내 말은 왜 ‘네’가 여기 있냐는 거야. 알바트로스가 아니라.

1200000651.english: I haven't seen any signs of campaigning recently, have I?

1200000652.english: Alpha. Did you know the AGS were voting?
1200000652.korean: 알파. AGS들이 선거하는 거 알고 있었어?

1200000653.english: I need to strengthen network security.

1200000656.english: We'll form an election management committee, so please hold the election again under the supervision of the committee.
1200000656.korean: 선거관리 위원회 구성할 테니까 위원회 입회하에 다시 선거해.

1200000657.english: I don't deny that AGS needs a representative, but the procedure should be appropriate.
1200000657.korean: AGS대표가 필요한 건 부정 안 하겠지만 절차는 적합해야지.

1200000658.english: I only listened for a moment, and I already found something inappropriate.
1200000658.korean: 내가 잠깐 듣기만 했는데도 부적절한 점을 찾았는데?

1200000659.english: A ball is a ball. The one who lives is the one who lives. Don't forget we're playing games together today.
1200000659.korean: 공은 공. 사는 사. 오늘 같이 게임하는 거 잊지 마.

1200000660.english: I accept gifts, but I don't accept bribes.
1200000660.korean: 나는 선물은 받아도 뇌물은 안 받아.

1200000661.english: Vanilla is also sharp-tongued with me, you know?

1200000662.english: Lover Lover, beyond that sky, so high!
1200000662.korean: Lover Lover 저 하늘 넘어 높-이!

1200000666.english: I'll fly, following you, into the endlessly stretching sky above!
1200000666.korean: 날아갈게 그대를 따라서 끝없이 펼쳐진 하늘 위로!

1200000667.english: ...It seems Sleipnir has gotten even dumber since Uroborus arrived.
1200000667.korean: ……우로보로스가 오고 난 후부터 슬레이프니르가 더 바보가 된 거 같아.

1200000668.english: Haha... Sleipnir must have had a lot of trouble leading the Sky Knights all this time.
1200000668.korean: 하하… 슬레이프니르가 지금까지 스카이 나이츠를 이끈다고 마음 고생이 심했잖아.

1200000669.english: Now that I can be spoiled, I'm going to be spoiled as much as I want.
1200000669.korean: 어리광을 부릴 수 있게 되니까 마음껏 어리광을 부리는 거지.

1200000670.english: I think we've had a harder time of it.
1200000670.korean: 마음고생은 우리가 더 한 거 같은데?

1200000674.english: Even tho the frequency of my foolishness has increased, the degree of it has decreased, hasn't it?
1200000674.korean: 그래도 바보스러움의 빈도는 늘어도 바보스러움의 정도는 줄었잖아.

1200000675.english: If it were the old pattern, you'd be kneeling in front of Blha and getting lectured about ten more times, right?
1200000675.korean: 옛날 패턴대로라면 블하 앞에 무릎 꿇고 잔소리를 열 번 정도는 더 들었을 걸?

1200000676.english: He's completely head over heels, just because Uroborus keeps telling him he's pretty and cute.
1200000676.korean: 얘도 우로보로스한테 예쁘다, 귀엽다 소리만 듣고 사니까 완전히 홀랑 넘어갔구만.

1200000677.english: They're all my lovely subordinates and disciples, so of course I can't help but find them adorable.
1200000677.korean: 전부 사랑스러운 부하이자 제자이니 예뻐 보일 수 밖에 없지 않나.

1200000678.english: Do you want to hear the griffin too? That's good.

1200000679.english: Griffons are brave, kind, and lovely. Always.

1200000680.english: I'm not doing this because I particularly want to hear that kind of thing!
1200000680.korean: 따, 딱히 그런 소리를 듣고 싶어서 이러는 건 아니거든!

1200000681.english: Hmph, and so what if it's foolish? If someone helps from the side, and if we think of foolishness as actions that go beyond the criminal's perception, wouldn't it sometimes lead to better results than the criminal themselves?
1200000681.korean: 후후, 그리고 바보스러우면 또 어떤가? 옆에서 도와주면 되고 또한 바보스러움이 범인의 인지를 벗어나는 행동을 한다는 의미로 생각하면 가끔은 범인보다 훌륭한 결과를 낼 때도 있지 않겠나?

1200000682.english: They say as long as you don't do anything bad, it's okay. Only bad things.
1200000682.korean: 나쁜 짓만 안 하면 된다네. 나쁜 짓만.

1200000683.english: Then there's nothing to worry about. That idiot isn't smart enough to do anything bad.
1200000683.korean: 그러면 걱정은 없겠네. 저 바보는 나쁜 짓을 저지를 정도로 똑똑하지 않으니까.

1200000684.english: No, why are you saying that being good means being foolish and unable to do bad things?
1200000684.korean: 아니, 착하다는 말을 두고 왜 바보스러우니 나쁜 짓을 못 저지른다고 말하는 거야!

1200000685.english: Hmph, don't get too angry, Sleipnir.

1200000686.english: Isn't it true that silly kids are often the cutest?
1200000686.korean: 원래 바보스러운 아이가 더 귀여운 법 아닌가.

1200000687.english: You're supposed to deny it in situations like this!

1200000691.english: Are there any other targets beside us showing up on the radar right now?
1200000691.korean: 지금 레이더 상에 우리 외의 다른 대상이 잡히는가?

1200000692.english: There's no one else but us, but... why do you ask?

1200000696.english: It's experiential, so it's difficult to explain in detail.

1200000699.english: It feels like the wind flow isn't normal.

1200000700.english: I will switch the radar to atmospheric observation mode.

1200000702.english: I'm sorry. I don't know what's wrong.

1200000703.english: Harpe. I will transmit the atmospheric observation information. Please analyze it.
1200000703.korean: 하르페. 대기 관측 정보를 전송하겠습니다. 분석 부탁드립니다.

1200000704.english: I'm not an expert in this either...

1200000705.english: But don't you have knowledge? You can do it.
1200000705.korean: 하지만 지식은 있지 않나? 자네라면 할 수 있네.

1200000711.english: - As I was talking to the team members loading relief supplies onto the transport vehicle, the emergency alarm suddenly went off.
1200000711.korean: - 내가 구호물자를 운송수단에 싣고 있는 대원들과 이야기를 나누고 있을 때 갑작스럽게 긴급 경보가 울렸다.

1200000712.english: - As soon as the alarm sounded, the crew members instantly surrounded me and went on alert.
1200000712.korean: - 대원들은 경보가 울리자마자 순식간에 나를 둘러 싸고 경계 태세에 들어갔다.

1200000713.english: - Before I could even open my mouth, a communication came in from Uroborus.
1200000713.korean: - 내가 입을 열기도 전에 우로보로스로부터 통신이 들어왔다.

1200000715.english: An unidentified flying object has appeared in the Orca airspace!

1200000716.english: Something huge is falling, something that can't be confirmed even on radar, let alone with the naked eye!
1200000716.korean: 통상 레이더 상으로도, 육안으로도 확인이 안 되는 거대한 무언가가 낙하하고 있네!

1200000717.english: We're not attacking because we haven't determined the object's intentions, but if it has hostile intentions, the damage will reach the commander's location!
1200000717.korean: 비행체의 의도가 파악되지 않은 상태이기에 공격은 안하고 있네만 이 비행체가 적대하는 의도가 있다면 피해가 사령관이 있는 곳까지 닿을 걸세!

1200000718.english: So I'll say it again! Evacuate!

1200000720.english: - The team members began evacuating me as they had been trained.
1200000720.korean: - 대원들은 훈련한 대로 나를 대피시키기 시작했다.

1200000721.english: - I, too, recognized the seriousness of the situation and obediently followed the other members.
1200000721.korean: - 나 역시도 사태의 심각함을 인지하고 순순히 대원들을 따랐다.

1200000722.english: Oh? Co-Commander, sir! Communication has come in from outside.
1200000722.korean: 아? 사, 사령관 각하! 외부에서 통신이 들어왔습니다.

1200000723.english: - Since it was highly likely, given the flow of events, that this communication came from the source of the commotion, I nodded.
1200000723.korean: - 흐름상 이 통신이 이 소란의 원인으로부터 왔을 확률이 높았기에 나는 고개를 끄덕였다.

1200000726.english: Oh, really? Just a moment. Have some water.
1200000726.korean: 아, 그래? 잠시만. 물 좀 마시고.

1200000727.english: I told you earlier I was going to turn on my microphone. You should drink water beforehand, before turning it on.
1200000727.korean: 마이크 켜겠다고 아까 말했잖아요. 물은 켜기 전에 미리 마시라고요.

1200000728.english: Now I'm being nagged even for drinking water.

1200000729.english: gulp! gulp! Honey... cough! Cough! Cough!
1200000729.korean: 꿀꺽! 꿀꺽! 꿀… 콜록! 콜록! 콜록!

1200000731.english: Cough! Choke! Cough! It's caught! Cough! Take it! Cough! Cough! What! Cough!
1200000731.korean: 콜록! 사레! 콜록! 걸린 거! 콜록! 가지고! 콜록! 콜록! 뭐라고! 콜록!

1200000732.english: ...I'm sorry, everyone in Orca. I will call you back in a moment.
1200000732.korean: ……죄송합니다, 오르카 여러분. 잠시 후에 다시 연락 드리겠습니다.

1200000735.english: - What is it? Why does the urgent situation just now feel like an overreaction?
1200000735.korean: - 뭐지? 긴박했던 방금 전의 상황이 호들갑처럼 느껴지는 건?

1200000736.english: Just because a fool is shooting doesn't mean it won't hurt, so let's finish evacuating, Master.
1200000736.korean: 바보가 쏘는 총이 안 아픈 건 아니니 일단 마저 대피하시죠, 주인님.

1200000737.english: - Inside Ferro, it seems the leader of the group who spoke with us has been declared a fool.
1200000737.korean: - 페로의 안에서는 우리와 대화를 나눈 집단의 대장이 바보라고 정해진 것 같다.

1200000738.english: - However, according to Uroborus' report, they are a group with unknown military power. Rather than doing nothing and getting caught off guard, it's better to take action, even if it means putting in some wasted effort.
1200000738.korean: - 하지만 우로보로스의 보고 대로라면 알 수 없는 무력을 지닌 집단이다. 아무런 대처를 안 하고 당하기보다는 헛된 수고를 들이더라도 대처를 하는 것이 좋겠지.

1200000739.english: - While directing the evacuation, I waited for the next communication.
1200000739.korean: - 대피를 지휘하며 나는 다음 통신을 기다렸다.

1200000740.english: - After about 10 minutes, communication was restored.
1200000740.korean: - 대충 10분이 흐른 후 다시 통신이 들어왔다.

1200000743.english: I am the admiral of the Golden Fleet and the master of the Dragon's Nest!
1200000743.korean: 황금 함대의 제독이자 용의 둥지의 주인이야!

1200000744.english: - I gestured for them to search for the names that had been heard from the communicator.
1200000744.korean: - 나는 손짓으로 통신기에서 들린 이름들을 검색하라고 신호를 보냈다.

1200000745.english: I have all the treasures of the world in my hands.
1200000745.korean: 나는 세상의 모든 보물을 손에 넣고 있지.

1200000746.english: And I've heard rumors about the last human, the most precious being in the world!
1200000746.korean: 그리고 나는 세상에서 가장 귀한 마지막 인간에 대한 소문을 들었어!

1200000747.english: All the treasures of the world are destined to be mine in the end!
1200000747.korean: 세상의 모든 보물은 결국 나의 것이 될 운명!

1200000748.english: So, the last human! Be mine! I won't allow refusal!
1200000748.korean: 그러니 마지막 인간! 내 것이 돼라! 거부는 허용하지 않을 거야!

1200000750.english: What is it? What's this idiot doing?

1200000751.english: - At any other time, Perrault would have been furious upon hearing the declaration of ownership, but now she was simply dumbfounded.
1200000751.korean: - 다른 때라면 나를 소유하겠다는 선언을 듣자마자 분노했을 페로는 그냥 어이없어하고 있었다.

1200000753.english: - To be honest... it's to the point where you could say it's a lack of safety awareness...
1200000753.korean: - 솔직하게 말하자면… 안전불감증이라고 말해도 될 정도이기는 하지만…

1200000754.english: - I didn't feel any particular sense of crisis either.

1200000755.english: - I thot they were putting on an act like that to catch us off guard, but...
1200000755.korean: - 우리를 방심시키기 위해서 저런 연기를 하나 싶었지만…

1200000756.english: It's completely different from the sentence I wrote in advance!
1200000756.korean: 미리 써 둔 문장이랑 완전히 다르잖아요!

1200000757.english: Are you crazy? Are you trying to start a fight right now? Are you sure?
1200000757.korean: 미쳤어요? 지금 싸우자는 거예요? 자신 있어요?

1200000758.english: If we fight Orca, I'll be the first to switch sides!?
1200000758.korean: 만약에 오르카랑 싸우면 제일 먼저 제가 전향해버릴 거예요!?

1200000760.english: The captain might not, but all the other crew members, including me, can't refuse a human's orders!
1200000760.korean: 대장은 몰라도 저 포함한 다른 승조원 전부 인간의 명령 거부 못 한다고요!

1200000761.english: If you want to die, die alone!

1200000765.english: But you said the master's authority doesn't work, right? I'm not sure, but that's how it sounded in context...
1200000765.korean: 하지만 주인님의 명령권이 통하지 않는다고 했었죠? 정확하지는 않지만 맥락이 그렇게 들렸는데…

1200000766.english: So that means it's a special model.

1200000768.english: - While Ferro was giving a scathing review, the search results popped up on my panel.
1200000768.korean: - 페로가 신랄한 평가를 내리는 동안에 검색 결과가 내 패널에 올라왔다.

1200000769.english: - Fafnir. A member of the Empress Hound.

1200000770.english: - The information provided by the Empress Hound members included information about Fafnir.
1200000770.korean: - 엠프레시스 하운드 멤버들이 제공한 정보에 파프니르의 정보가 있었다.

1200000772.english: - Ferro changed her attitude in an instant.

1200000773.english: - You must be remembering the battle with JangHwa in Yokohama and the battle with the Vargr in Svalbard.
1200000773.korean: - 요코하마에서 장화와의 전투, 스발바르 제도에서 바르그와의 전투를 떠올린 것이겠지.

1200000774.english: - The members of the Empress Hounds are specialized in irregular warfare such as terrorism or assassination, and can inflict significant damage even when operating alone.
1200000774.korean: - 테러 혹은 암살 같은 비정규전 등에 특화되어 단독으로도 큰 피해를 입힐 수 있는 게 엠프레시스 하운드의 구성원들이다.

1200000775.english: - Regardless of their attitude, you can't let your guard down against an Empress Hound.
1200000775.korean: - 태도는 어찌되었건 간에 엠프레시스 하운드라면 방심할 수 없다.

1200000776.english: - I signaled for the Empress Hound members to be summoned.
1200000776.korean: - 나는 엠프레시스 하운드 멤버들을 소집하라고 신호를 보냈다.

1200000777.english: I'm sorry, everyone at Orca. And finally, the last human.
1200000777.korean: 죄송합니다, 오르카의 여러분. 그리고 마지막 인간님.

1200000778.english: Please disregard our leader's recent remarks as just the ramblings of a fool.
1200000778.korean: 저희 대장의 방금 발언은 그냥 멍청이의 헛소리로 치부해주시길 바랍니다.

1200000780.english: It's better to publicly declare the captain is an idiot than to fight Orca!
1200000780.korean: 대장이 멍청이라고 공언하는 쪽이 오르카랑 싸우는 것보다 나아요!

1200000782.english: I'm sorry, Orca people. I will call you back in a moment.
1200000782.korean: 죄송합니다, 오르카 여러분. 잠시 후에 다시 연락 드리겠습니다.

1200000783.english: - ...An Empress Hound would be very dangerous.

1200000784.english: - Yeah... because that's how it's been so far... it'll be dangerous.

1200000787.english: That's too harsh, calling me an idiot...

1200000788.english: It must feel bad to be the one listening.

1200000791.english: Even if someone isn't present, it's not desirable to speak ill of them.
1200000791.korean: 아무리 자리에 없다고 하더라도 다른 사람 욕을 하는 건 바람직하지 못하다.

1200000792.english: Fafnir is a Bioroid developed based on Black River's bioelectric technology.
1200000792.korean: 파프니르는 블랙리버 생체전기 기술을 기반하여 개발된 바이오로이드입니다.

1200000793.english: Specialized in infiltrating power plants or substations to plunge an entire city into darkness, or, on a smaller scale, hiding within specific facilities to overload and erase information from their electronic devices.
1200000793.korean: 발전소 혹은 변전소에 잠입하여 도시 하나를 정전시키거나 작게는 특정 시설에 숨어들어 시설의 전자기기들을 과부하시켜 정보를 말소시키는 데에 특화되었습니다.

1200000794.english: ...Although their thinking ability is limited, it is sufficient for them to carry out their original mission.
1200000794.korean: ……비록 생각하는 능력은 부족하지만 자신의 본래 임무를 수행하는 데에는 충분합니다.

1200000795.english: Huh? It's impossible for that idiot...

1200000796.english: No... never mind. In the old days, Yeomong was considered foolish, but his scholarship advanced rapidly, and it was said that if a scholar didn't see him for three days, they would rub their eyes and have to look again.
1200000796.korean: 아니… 아니군요. 그 옛날 여몽도 아둔하다는 평가를 받았지만 학문이 일취월장하여 선비는 사흘을 못 보면 눈을 비비고 다시 봐야한다는 말을 했지요.

1200000797.english: Since it's been a long time since we've seen each other, you might have discovered the joy of learning in the meantime.
1200000797.korean: 못 본 세월이 기니 그 동안 배움의 즐거움을 깨달았을 수도 있겠군요.

1200000798.english: - It seems the evaluations from my old colleagues are no different...
1200000798.korean: - 옛 동료들의 평가도 다를 게 없구나…

1200000800.english: I apologize for saying this after already entering your airspace, but would you grant us permission to enter your airspace?
1200000800.korean: 이미 영공을 진입한 상태에서 이런 말 하기 죄송스럽지만… 영공 진입을 허락해 주시겠습니까?

1200000801.english: We have absolutely no intention of being hostile toward Orca.
1200000801.korean: 저희는 오르카와 적대할 의사가 전혀 없습니다.

1200000802.english: - I looked around. The fleet led by Fafnir was nowhere to be seen.
1200000802.korean: - 나는 주위를 둘러보았다. 파프니르가 이끈다는 함대는 보이지 않았다.

1200000804.english: - The moment I said that, it suddenly appeared.
1200000804.korean: - 내가 그렇게 말한 순간 그것은 갑자기 모습을 드러냈다.

1200000805.english: - It was far too large to be called a flying object. It was incredibly large, thick, and long, and it was simple. It was literally...
1200000805.korean: - 그것은 비행체라고 하기엔 너무도 컸다. 엄청나게 크고 두껍고 길었으며, 그리고 단순했다. 그것은 말 그대로…

1200000806.english: - Seeing its unrealistic size, we were overwhelmed.
1200000806.korean: - 비현실적인 크기의 그것을 보고 우리는 압도되었다.

1200000807.english: - Regardless of our attitude, a calm request came thru the communicator.
1200000807.korean: - 그런 우리의 태도에 아랑곳없이 통신기에서는 무덤덤한 요청이 전해졌다.

1200000809.english: Would you grant us permission to enter Orca airspace?

1200000812.english: Leave everything behind and get out of there first!
1200000812.korean: 모든 물자는 내버려두고 우선 현장을 떠나!

1200000814.english: Can you tell me about Fafnir?

1200000815.english: But it looks like that Fafnir is leading a group, doesn't it?
1200000815.korean: 그런데 그 파프니르가 한 집단을 이끄는 거 같은데?

1200000816.english: Uroborus. What is the location of the aircraft?

1200000820.english: Would you grant us permission to enter Orca airspace?

1200000821.english: - His tone was polite, but in front of a colossal flying object, any words would become a threat.
1200000821.korean: - 말투는 정중했지만 압도적인 크기의 비행체를 앞에 두면 어떤 말이든 협박이 되고 만다.

1200000822.english: - I remembered a word I had learned from Alexandra before.
1200000822.korean: - 나는 전에 알렉산드라에게 배웠던 단어를 떠올렸다.

1200000823.english: - Inclusive diplomacy. A foreign policy where a person with strong military power flaunts their strength and leads negotiations to their advantage.
1200000823.korean: - 포함외교. 강대한 무력을 갖춘 자가 무력을 과시하며 자신에게 유리하게 협상을 이끄는 외교 정책.

1200000824.english: - If we refuse to allow them to enter our airspace now, will they simply back down?
1200000824.korean: - 지금 영공 진입을 허용하지 않겠다고 하면 저들은 순순히 물러날까?

1200000825.english: - And when Fafnir introduced herself, she said she was the fleet’s admiral.
1200000825.korean: - 그리고 파프니르는 자신을 소개할 때 함대의 제독이라고 말했다.

1200000826.english: - If it's a fleet, that means there are more than just that flying object.
1200000826.korean: - 함대라면 저 비행체 외에도 더 있다는 의미일 것이다.

1200000827.english: - How could a group with such massive flying objects have remained unknown until now?
1200000827.korean: - 저렇게 거대한 비행체를 가지고 있는 집단이 어떻게 지금까지 알려지지 않았지?

1200000829.english: - My consciousness, which had been submerged in thot by Ferro's voice, surfaced.
1200000829.korean: - 페로의 목소리에 생각에 매몰되던 의식이 부상했다.

1200000830.english: Seeing Ferro's worried expression, I deliberately smiled.
1200000830.korean: - 걱정이 가득한 페로의 표정을 보고 나는 일부러 웃어 보였다.

1200000831.english: - If I'm anxious, everyone else gets anxious too.

1200000832.english: Yes, I will inform them that we are granting permission to enter the airspace.

1200000833.english: - Habetrot operated the communicator and relayed my message.
1200000833.korean: - 하베트롯이 통신기를 조작하며 내 메시지를 전달했다.

1200000835.english: We have absolutely no intention of being hostile toward Orca, and what our captain said was-
1200000835.korean: 저희는 오르카를 적대할 의사가 전혀 없으며, 저희 대장이 했던 말은-

1200000839.english: What were you doing, not stopping them!

1200000842.english: Even if I wanted to be nervous, I just can't.

1200000843.english: - As we were dumbfounded, something approached us from afar.
1200000843.korean: - 우리가 어이없어 할 때 저 멀리서 무언가가 우리에게 다가왔다.

1200000847.english: - After a long while, something finally arrived in front of us.
1200000847.korean: - 한참 후에 무언가는 간신히 우리 앞에 도착했다.

1200000848.english: - The crew members blocked my path.

1200000849.english: - Regardless, the person who approached us was furious.
1200000849.korean: - 그러거나 말거나 우리에게 다가온 그 자는 역정을 냈다.

1200000850.english: Why is it so far away!

1200000851.english: - Since it was the voice I had heard from the communicator, I asked.

1200000853.english: The admiral of the Golden Fleet and the master of the Dragon's Nest. Fafnir, who will become the master of all the treasures in this world.
1200000853.korean: 황금 함대의 제독이자 용의 둥지의 주인. 이 세상 모든 보물의 주인이 될 파프니르야.

1200000854.english: You're the last human, aren't you?

1200000855.english: Why are you drinking at the same level as me, Master...?

1200000857.english: Me too. The Great Conjunction Expert, the Master of Tesla Tail Jupiter, and... Um... and...
1200000857.korean: 나, 나도. 대정전 전문가, 테슬라 테일 주피터의 주인 그리고. 어… 그리고…

1200000859.english: - Ferro was looking at Fafnir, who was revealing her true identity out of a sense of rivalry toward her, with pitying eyes. It was a gaze that didn't convey any sense of crisis at all.
1200000859.korean: - 나에 대한 대항심으로 자신의 정체를 늘어놓는 파프니르를 페로가 짠한 눈으로 바라보고 있었다. 위기감이 전혀 느껴지지 않는 시선이었다.

1200000860.english: Um, yeah. A member of the Empress Hound!

1200000861.english: Do you know what an Empress Hound is?

1200000868.english: I told you not to call it a battery!

1200000869.english: You've grown a lot while I wasn't looking, Babonir?
1200000869.korean: 못 보는 사이에 많이 컸다, 바보니르?

1200000870.english: Don't call people by the wrong names! And I'm taller than you!
1200000870.korean: 함부로 남의 이름 바꿔서 부르지 말라고! 그리고 키는 내가 너보다 더 커!

1200000871.english: Is that big thing floating over there yours?
1200000871.korean: 저기 떠 있는 커다란 게 네 거야?

1200000872.english: Hmph, alright. This is the dragon's nest in my sky fortress.
1200000872.korean: 후후, 그래. 내 공중 요새 용의 둥지야.

1200000873.english: Even if you work hard your whole life, you'll never get something like that, will you?
1200000873.korean: 너 같은 건 평생 노력해도 저런 건 못 구할 걸?

1200000875.english: Can I borrow it for a little while? I'll just use it a little and give it back.

1200000876.english: You said that before too, and you borrowed a parachute from me and then just jumped without returning it!
1200000876.korean: 너 옛날에도 그 말 하고 나한테서 낙하산 빌려 갔다가 안 돌려주고 그냥 뛰어내렸잖아!

1200000877.english: I almost died jumping without a parachute!
1200000877.korean: 나는 낙하산 없이 뛰어내리느라 죽을 뻔했다고!

1200000878.english: Hey, what's wrong? Between friends? As long as they didn't die, that's all that matters.
1200000878.korean: 에이, 왜 그래. 친구끼리? 안 죽었으면 됐잖아.

1200000879.english: That's funny! When were we ever friends?

1200000880.english: I'm not quite happy with you, are you? When did you ever think you were on our level?
1200000880.korean: 너 조금 마음에 안 든다? 네가 언제 우리랑 맞먹었냐?

1200000881.english: And isn't it a good thing that you have to report it to this older sister first?
1200000881.korean: 그리고 좋은 건 이 언니한테 먼저 신고해야 하는 거 아냐?

1200000882.english: - I guess JangHwa and CheonA are doing this because Fafnir isn't a member of Orca, right?
1200000882.korean: - 장화와 천아도 파프니르가 오르카의 일원이 아니라서 이러는 거겠지?

1200000883.english: - You're doing this because it's not true, right?

1200000884.english: Hey, isn't it better to just lend it and get hit a few times than to not lend it at all? We don't mind either way, tho.
1200000884.korean: 야, 그냥 빌려주는 게 몇 대 맞고 빌려주는 것보다 낫지 않냐? 우리는 어느 쪽이든 상관없지만.

1200000885.english: - ...Let's ask Alexandra to open an etiquette class later.

1200000886.english: It's in front of the master. Don't make a fool of yourself.
1200000886.korean: 주인님 앞이다. 흉한 꼴 보이지 마라.

1200000887.english: - As Vargr intervened, the commotion subsided in an instant.

1200000888.english: - Just as I was admiring myself, thinking, "I am indeed the leader of the Empress Hounds..."
1200000888.korean: - 내가 역시 엠프레시스 하운드의 대장이구나 하고 감탄하고 있을 때…

1200000890.english: - I felt the previously relaxed atmosphere instantly turn cold.
1200000890.korean: - 가라앉았던 분위기가 순식간에 차가워지는 게 느껴졌다.

1200000892.english: I am Vargr, the leader of the Empress Hounds.

1200000893.english: What? The Empress Hound has a leader? And you're the captain? That's funny.
1200000893.korean: 뭐? 엠프레시스 하운드에 대장이 있다고? 그리고 그 대장이 너라고? 웃기시네.

1200000894.english: - I saw CheonA, who was next to Vargr, raise her index finger. What does that mean?
1200000894.korean: - 바르그 옆에 있던 천아가 검지를 세우는 것이 보였다. 무슨 의미지?

1200000895.english: Don't you think I'm more suited to be the boss than a little kid like you?
1200000895.korean: 너 같은 꼬맹이보다는 내가 더 대장에 어울리지 않아?

1200000896.english: It's due to my own shortcomings. I will strive to improve.

1200000897.english: - CheonA raised her middle finger after her index finger. I noticed CheonA was counting.
1200000897.korean: - 천아가 검지에 이어 중지를 세웠다. 나는 천아가 카운트를 하고 있다는 것을 알아차렸다.

1200000898.english: You can barely lead these ill-tempered guys, but I have far more subordinates, treasures, and even a dragon's nest.
1200000898.korean: 너는 기껏해야 이 성격 더러운 애들이나 이끌지만 나는 훨씬 많은 부하에 보물이랑, 용의 둥지도 가지고 있다고.

1200000899.english: I'll give you credit for that. It will be proof that you also lived diligently, even after humanity perished.
1200000899.korean: 그건 칭찬하마. 인류가 멸망한 후에도 너 역시도 열심히 살았다는 증거일 테니.

1200000900.english: - CheonA also spread out her medicine bag. At least Vargr's yellow card count is more than three.
1200000900.korean: - 천아가 약지도 펼쳤다. 적어도 바르그의 옐로카드는 3장보다는 많다는 의미겠지.

1200000903.english: - CheonA spread her arms wide. It meant she had touched the Vargr's Achilles' heel, something that didn't even need to be counted.
1200000903.korean: - 천아는 양손을 쫙 펼쳤다. 카운트를 할 필요도 없는 바르그의 역린을 건드렸다는 의미였다.

1200000904.english: - I hugged the Warg from behind as it reached for its sword.
1200000904.korean: - 나는 칼을 뽑으려 드는 바르그를 뒤에서 끌어안았다.

1200000905.english: Lord, Master? In a place like this?

1200000907.english: Seeing Vargr, whose face had turned red for a different reason, CheonA was dumbfounded.
1200000907.korean: - 다른 의미로 얼굴을 붉힌 바르그를 보며 천아는 어이없어 했다.

1200000909.english: - And not far away, a group of people we'd never seen before came running toward us...
1200000909.korean: - 그리고 멀지 않은 곳에서 처음보는 무리가 우리에게 달려오다가…

1200000910.english: - I stopped in my tracks when I saw the Orca crew.

1200000911.english: - However, one of them didn't stop and ran all the way to Fafnir's side.
1200000911.korean: - 그러나 그 중 한 명은 발걸음을 멈추지 않고 파프니르의 옆까지 달려왔다.

1200000913.english: Our leader isn't doing this out of malice, but simply because she's lacking. Please forgive her with a generous heart!
1200000913.korean: 저희 대장이 악의가 있어서 그런 게 아니라 어디까지나 부족해서 그런 것이니 너른 마음으로 부디 용서해주세요!

1200000914.english: Hey, why is it assumed that I'm the one who's wrong?
1200000914.korean: 야, 왜 내가 잘못했다는 게 전제야!

1200000915.english: Can't you feel the bad atmosphere right now?
1200000915.korean: 지금 분위기가 안 좋은 거 못 느껴요?

1200000916.english: I've told you countless times, we can't win against Orca, even if we die!
1200000916.korean: 오르카랑 싸우면 우리는 죽어도 못 이긴다고 몇 번이나 말씀드렸어요!

1200000918.english: Then what's this atmosphere right now?

1200000919.english: I just came down to see the last human who will be my treasure!
1200000919.korean: 그냥 내 보물이 될 마지막 인간을 직접 보려고 내려왔을 뿐이라고!

1200000920.english: This is driving me crazy, seriously.

1200000921.english: Command me, Master. I will offer that woman's head.
1200000921.korean: 명하십시오, 주인님. 저 년의 목을 베어바치겠습니다.

1200000922.english: - I stroked Vargr's head and soothed her.

1200000924.english: And are you in your right mind? What nerve do you have to come down here alone!
1200000924.korean: 그리고 제정신이세요? 무슨 배짱으로 혼자서 내려오냐고요!

1200000925.english: - I noticed that the voice of the person fighting Fafnir matched the voice I heard from the communicator.
1200000925.korean: - 나는 파프니르와 싸우는 사람의 목소리가 통신기에서 들려오던 목소리와 일치한다는 것을 알아차렸다.

1200000926.english: - The bio-droid, who had been bickering with Fafnir, straightened up when I asked.
1200000926.korean: - 파프니르와 티격태격하던 바이오로이드는 내가 묻자 자세를 바로 했다.

1200000927.english: Hello, last human. I'm Sterling, the head of the Haka.
1200000927.korean: 안녕하세요, 마지막 인간님. 하카의 기관장인 스털링이라고 합니다.

1200000928.english: "Haka" sounds uncool! It's a dragon's nest!

1200000929.english: - Sterling continued, her expression one of barely suppressed exasperation.
1200000929.korean: - 스털링은 한숨을 쉬고 싶은 것을 억지로 참는 표정으로 말을 이었다.

1200000930.english: Yes. This is the high-altitude transport airship that Black River was secretly developing. It's an abbreviation for High Altitude Cargo Airship.
1200000930.korean: 예. 블랙리버에서 비밀리에 개발하고 있던 고고도 수송비행선입니다. High Altitude Cargo Airship의 약자죠.

1200000931.english: Our boss wants to call it the Dragon's Nest, but its original name is Haka. Humanity was wiped out before the naming ceremony even happened.
1200000931.korean: 저희 대장은 용의 둥지라고 부르고 싶어하지만… 원래 이름은 하카입니다. 명명식을 열기도 전에 인류가 전부 멸망했거든요.

1200000932.english: So, I gave it a name, right? How cool is that?
1200000932.korean: 그러니까 내가 이름을 지어줬잖아. 얼마나 멋져?

1200000933.english: Right? Right? Heh heh, just as expected of the last human, you've got good taste, don't you?
1200000933.korean: 그렇지? 그렇지? 후후, 역시 마지막 인간답게 센스 있잖아?

1200000935.english: If you keep talking nonsense, am I going to switch to Orca?

1200000936.english: Don't do that~. If you leave, who will move the dragon's nest?
1200000936.korean: 그러지 마~. 네가 떠나면 용의 둥지를 누가 움직여?

1200000937.english: Our boss said she wants to see the last human...
1200000937.korean: 저희 대장이 마지막 인간님을 보고 싶다고 해서…

1200000938.english: That dared to claim the master as its own?
1200000938.korean: 저것은 감히 주인님을 자기 것으로 만들겠다고 했는데?

1200000941.english: Just... think of it as if you're here on vacation. Even if you have the intention but lack the ability and don't try, it's not a sin, is it?
1200000941.korean: 그냥… 관광 왔다고 생각해 주세요. 의도는 있어도 능력이 없고 시도하지 않으면 죄가 되는 건 아니잖아요.

1200000942.english: - Sterling finally couldn't hold back and sighed.
1200000942.korean: - 스털링은 결국 참지 못하고 한숨을 내쉬었다.

1200000943.english: And I'd like to get some help too.

1200000944.english: The function of the haka is malfunctioning, and I'd like to have it serviced. However, our manpower alone isn't enough...
1200000944.korean: 하카의 기능에 이상이 생겨서 정비를 하고 싶거든요. 그런데 저희들 인력만으로는 부족해서…

1200000945.english: Of course, I'm not asking for help for free.

1200000946.english: We've collected quite a few treasures, you know.

1200000947.english: Our proud. The boss has a hobby of collecting things that sparkle like crows.
1200000947.korean: 저희 자랑스러운. 대장께서 까마귀처럼 반짝이는 걸 바리바리 모으는 취미가 있거든요.

1200000948.english: In this age, useless treasures, worth less than a single loaf of bread.
1200000948.korean: 이 시대에, 빵 하나보다 가치 없는, 쓸 데 없는 보물들을 말이죠.

1200000949.english: Hey! My treasure is not allowed!

1200000950.english: Captain, Haka's current condition is entirely your fault!
1200000950.korean: 대장, 지금 하카가 상태가 안 좋은 건 전적으로 대장 때문이에요!

1200000951.english: The treasure is so heavy that it's overloading Haka!
1200000951.korean: 보물 무게가 너무 무거워서 하카에 과부하가 걸린 상태라고요!

1200000952.english: - I recalled the information I had heard thru Wristcut.
1200000952.korean: - 나는 리스트컷을 통해서 들은 정보를 떠올렸다.

1200000953.english: - I heard there's a shortage of gold recently...

1200000954.english: More than recently... I've been collecting them ever since I met the captain. The captain seems to have been collecting them even before that.
1200000954.korean: 최근이라기보다는… 제가 대장을 만난 이후로 계속 모으고 있었죠. 대장은 그 전부터 모으고 있었던 것 같지만.

1200000956.english: Shall I show you? Don't you want to see it? Then follow me. I'll show you my treasure!
1200000956.korean: 보여줄까? 보고 싶지? 그러면 따라와. 내 보물을 보여줄게!

1200000959.english: I cannot allow my master to be in any danger, even the slightest.
1200000959.korean: 주인님께서 조금이라도 위험한 일에 처하게 둘 수 없다.

1200000960.english: As soon as I get on that airship, they could harm the master or just run away. Or you could blow up the airship.
